what_it_does: Records metadata for a design-source channel image asset named recent-pitches/p-01 and labels it as source design ground-truth.
observations: YAML frontmatter stores an id, address, channel, path, blob URI, MIME type, dimensions, source, author session, creation timestamp, and an empty links list.
The image dimensions are recorded as 0x0.
The body states the file is 'SOURCE design ground-truth — recent-pitches/p-01.jpg'.
standouts: Width and height are both zero.
conventions: Ledger-style metadata file with YAML frontmatter and a short plain-text body.
Uses image:// and blob:// URI schemes for asset addressing.
Follows a channel-memory naming convention: image__<channel>_<path>.md.
concerns: Zero width and height may indicate missing or uncomputed image dimensions.
The 'links' list is empty, so no explicit relationships are recorded for this asset.
notes: This is a ledger card for a single design-source image pitch asset, flagged as ground-truth but lacking recorded dimensions or outbound links.
questions: What does 'recent-pitches' classify in the design-source channel?
Is the blob URI resolvable and what does the image depict?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to catalog a source image asset with provenance; it does so, but the zero dimensions and empty links mean the catalog entry is incomplete.
intention_signals: It tries to create a trustworthy, addressable record of a design pitch image so downstream systems can locate and verify the original source material.
contribution: Provides a stable, URI-addressable metadata record that links a logical image identifier to a content-addressed blob and a provenance label.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory ledger entry for the design-source image 'recent-pitches/p-01', storing its id, blob reference, MIME type, dimensions, author session, creation time, and a 'SOURCE design ground-truth' label.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and ground-truth a single design pitch image in the design-source channel for later retrieval and provenance.
